notes: It formerly didn't work but after clearing my prefix and starting anew it now does work!  I think there was a UPlay update that allows this but that is just a guess.  Anyhow now on first launch it will install dependency software and last will be UPlay. The provided old UPlay will launch and then update twice and then ask for login. After logging in I suggest turning off all UPlay notifications and overlays because they will display but be unresponsive and thus impossible to dismiss during gaming.  After this close the window.  The next time you launch through Steam it WILL work, launching UPlay and then the game.  It works BOTH with DX9 Wine D3D renderer or DXVK DX11 renderer, your choice.  There is in DX11 some strange color bleeding bug but otherwise works without issues.  DX9 version no bugs I have detected. The last issue is it does not close cleanly, it is necessary to close upc.exe process manually after exiting.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Same poster as above.  I should have added that installing lib32-gnutls was required for UPlay.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: With newest Steam Play, 3.16-8 Beta, the color bleed bug is gone while using DX11 (and DX9 never had that bug).  Didn't encounter any artifacts and also good performance.  The only remaining issue is the need to close upc.exe process manually after exiting.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Ran perfectly out of the box with no additional tweaks required. Played the game from start to finish.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: It worked pretty much flawlessly. The only issues I had were due to the way Steam interacts with Uplay. I couldn't launch it directly from Uplay, and after registering the key, I had two instances of Blood Dragon in my library (one was a Uplay freebie). But once I disabled overlays and notifications in Uplay (as per someone else's recommendation) synced my Steam login to Uplay, and picked the correct Blood Dragon, it launched without a hitch. There are some framerate drops, but that could be because of my system being a bit slow. I remember Far Cry 3 also not running that well, albeit on a much slower CPU.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: (Proton 4.2-1) Had to disable Uplay overlay first for the game to start, also I had 2 versions of far cry 3 blood dragon in uplay, for me i have to select the bottom one when uplay asks which one to launch. Uplay also continues running in the tray, making steam think the game is still running, so you have to first quit uplay before starting the game from steam again. Performance seems alright
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Was able to complete it, including all achievements and Uplay club rewards. Required several tweaks: First of all, Uplay game overlay had to be disabled. After the game has been closed, all .exe's had to be killed manually. I also experienced crashing upon save load or death.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Disable Uplay overlay.  OOTB No Issues.  No other workarounds were needed.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Doesn't work out of the box on Proton, you need the typical Uplay workarounds. Restart Uplay several times, login, enter a key, DISABLE Uplay overlay and then the game worked fine with DXVK.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Game starts and runs after UPlay-bypassing crack is installed. FPS is lower than it would be on Windows and there is some mild audio cracking. Seems to work great otherwise.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon

notes: Disable Uplay in-game overlay and notifications, and the game works pretty much perfect. Performance not quite as good as in Windows as usual, but still perfectly smooth and playable on my machine. Solid Proton title.
title: Far Cry 3 - Blood Dragon
